entries.definition:
-ʾ-n-m (no verb) all God’s creatures.
أَنَام ʾanām [collect. n. occurring once in the Qur’an] creatures (including humankind. Some philologists describe it as meaning every creature that goes to sleep, as if it were derived from ن/و/م n-w-m) (55:10) وَالْأَرْضَ وَضَعَهَا لِلْأَنَامِ and the Earth He laid down for

59

أنّى

the creatures.

أَنَّى ʾannā a sister of إِنَّ ʾinna (من أخوات إنّ) (q.v.) a subordinating particle occurring some 360 times in the Qur’an. Like إِنَّ it lends emphasis to the context but differs from it in the fact that أَنَّ, together with the following sentence, undergoes a process of subordination and functions as a nominal clause serving as, e.g., a subject (72:1) أُوحِيَ إِلَيَّ أَنَّهُ اسْتَمَعَ نَفَرٌ مِّنَ الْجِنِّ [it] has been revealed to me that a company of the jinn listened in [on a recitation of the Qur’an] or as an object (6:109) وَمَا يُشْعِرُكُمْ أَنَّهَا إِذَا جَاءَتْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ what will make you realise that if it [a sign from God] came [to them] they still will not believe?

إِنَّ ʾinna emphatic particle (حرف تحقيق وتأكيد), occurring some 1679 times in the Qur’an. It is the main member of a group of five particles designated by Arab grammarians as ‘ʾinna and its sisters’ (إنّ وأخواتها) (إنّ ʾinna, أنَّ ʾanna, كأنّ kaʾanna, لعلّ laʿalla and ليت layta), which precede nominal sentences governing the subject in the accusative case and lend various semantic implications, particular to each particle, to the contexts in which they appear. (see particle alphabetically). Besides functioning as ‘certainly’, an emphatic particle carrying the force of ‘indeed’, ‘surely’ (36:16) إِنَّا إِلَيْكُمْ لَمُرْسَلُونَ indeed we have been sent to you; (49:12) إِنَّ بَعْضَ الظَّنِّ [even] some suspicion is a sin; إِنَّ may also function as a particle of causation (حرف تعليل) (12:53) وَمَا أُبَرِّئُ نَفْسِي إِنَّ النَّفْسَ لَأَمَّارَةٌ بِالسُّوءِ I do not exonerate myself for man’s very soul incites him to evil.

إِنَّمَا ʾinnamā (said by Arab grammarians to be a compound of the emphatic إِنَّ + so-called ‘redundant’ ما which prevents إِنَّ having any government) particle of limitation (إِفَادَةُ حَصْرٍ أَوْ قَصْرٍ), occurring some 22 times in the Qur’an; so-called because it denotes restriction of that which it precedes to that which follows it, ‘x is only y’ (13:7) إِنَّمَا أَنتَ مُنذِرٌ you are but a warner.

أَنَّى ʾannā I interrogative (اسم استفهام) occurring 28 times in the Qur’an. In addition to the element of strong exclamation it lends to the

whole context, أَنّى denotes the senses of: 1 ‘however?!’ (19:20) أَنّىٰ يَكُونُ لِي غُلَٰمٌ وَلَمْ يَمْسَسْنِي بَشَرٌ however can I have a son when no mortal has touched me?! 2 ‘wherever from?!’ (3:37) يَٰمَرْيَمُ أَنّىٰ لَكِ هَٰذَا Mary, from wherever [on earth] do you get these [provisions]?! II adverbial (ظرف) ‘whenever’ as in one interpretation of (2:223) نِسَآؤُكُمْ حَرْثٌ لَّكُمْ فَأْتُوا۟ حَرْثَكُمْ أَنّىٰ شِئْتُمْ your women are your fields, so go into your fields whenever you like.
